title: Classification of Points in a Metric Space
description: A Venn diagram showing the relationship between interior, exterior, and boundary points of a set A within a metric space X.
relationships: An exterior point has a neighborhood contained entirely in the complement of A (A^c). A boundary point has neighborhoods that always contain points both in A and in A^c. The entire space X is partitioned into the interior of A, the exterior of A, and the boundary of A.
